---
title: "Analyzing Water Quality Data"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 1."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-5
---

**This module is designed to be used in conjunction with the Lesson 5 slide deck and other resources located in the facilitator's guide.**

::: {.gray-container}

**Lesson Objective:** In this lesson, you will use AI to ask questions of and analyze water quality data.

**Materials Needed:**  
- Your computer  
- A web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Safari)    
:::

## Recalling Previous Workshops
 - In workshop #1, we learned about introductory statistics and the Python programming language 
 - In workshop #2, we learned how to use an AI classification model and how the buoy uses this kind of model to check for new test pads!
 - In workshop #3, we learned about how to use an AI regression model to predict nitrate levels from RGB color values on test strips.
 - In workshop #4, we learned about physical computing and how tiny computers, other physical components, and computer code can come together to make really great things—like marshmellow sorters!

## Today: We'll put all this together and analyze the data from Smokey Buoy!

![](images/smokey_buoy.png){fig-align="center"}

Let's get started!
